title: Replacement strings should reference existing regular expression groups
description: Replacement strings should reference existing regular expression groups. This helps to maintain consistency and readability.
message: Replacement string does not reference an existing group.
recommendation: Use pre-defined groups in regular expressions.

noncompliant.code:
import re
pattern = re.compile(r'pattern(.*?)replacement')
result = pattern.sub('replacement', 'input')
noncompliant.description: Replacement string does not reference a group.

why_is_this_an_issue: Replacement strings that do not reference existing regular expression groups can lead to inconsistencies and make the code harder to maintain.
how_to_fix: Ensure replacement strings reference existing regular expression groups using the '(?P<name>...)' syntax.

metadata.title: Replacement strings should reference existing regular expression groups
